reasoning: The mathematical conditions for a YES resolution require the number of battles in the 30-day target period (Feb 20 - Mar 21) to be strictly greater than the 30-day average over the preceding 360-day baseline (Mar 20, 2025 - Mar 14, 2026). The historical 360-day average up to Mar 5 was 10 battles (0.833 average). We have definitively confirmed that on March 12 and 13, 2026, the Algerian army had two separate violent clashes with militants in Tebessa, resulting in 10 deaths (3 soldiers, 7 militants). Since both occurred within both the 30-day target period and the 360-day target baseline, they will contribute to both. Assuming these count as at least 1 'Battle' (likely 2 per ACLED methodology), the focal count Y is >= 1. The new baseline total X would be at most 10 + 1 = 11 (if Y=1). In this limiting scenario, Y (1) is strictly greater than X/12 (11/12 = 0.916). If Y=2, Y (2) > 12/12 = 1.0. For the question to resolve NO, ACLED would have to retroactively add missing events from the past year to push the baseline above 11 while simultaneously coding the recent events as only a single battle, an extremely improbable combination of unlikely circumstances.
